title: Ignore Rules, Attributes, Line Endings, and Repository Content · Graph Lens: Attributes Effects
summary: Keep generated output, local configuration, binaries, and secrets out of history while preserving cross-platform text behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git attributes effects increment in a content-boundary and normalization policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use attributes for text normalization, diff, merge, export, and binary behavior with check-attr evidence.
Choose repository ignore, info/exclude, and global excludes from whether a rule belongs to every collaborator or one environment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

Apply that move specifically to Attributes Effects while producing a content-boundary and normalization policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-attributes-effects — Use attributes for text normalization, diff, merge, export, and binary behavior with check-attr evidence.
# Evidence: changed Git case 3
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
